questions.question: What percentage of positive replies go unanswered within 24 hours?
questions.explanation: 30-50% of positive replies go unanswered within 24 hours. This is the biggest revenue leak in outbound — reply routing automation fixes it.

questions.question: What is the most critical step in the reply routing automation?
questions.explanation: Pausing active sequences is the most critical step. A positive reply followed by a scheduled follow-up email (because the sequence kept running) is one of the most damaging automation failures.

questions.question: AI reply classification should be used to automatically send responses without human review.
questions.explanation: False. AI classification accuracy is 85-95% — good for routing (creating different tasks) but not reliable enough for auto-responding. Always keep human review in the response step.

questions.question: Which reply type requires the LOWEST urgency response task?

options.text: Pricing question with team size specified
Out-of-office with return date in 90 days

questions.explanation: Out-of-office with a 90-day return date requires a scheduled follow-up task, not an immediate response. The other three all indicate active engagement that warrants a 2-4 hour response.
